name: Thai Glass Noodles (Yam Woon Sen)
recipeIngredient: 1 Whole chicken breast, boned, skinned and coarsely chopped
1 Fresh red or green chile, chopped
6 ounces Cooked bay shrimp, shredded lettuce
recipeInstructions:
This recipe comes from the Oriental Hotel in Bangkok, location of the Thai Cooking School. It has been adapted for the American cook. With this recipe it's necessary to use mung bean glass noodles rather than those based on rice flour. Check the ingredients on the package when buying.
1. Put mung bean noodles in a bowl and pour in lukewarm water to cover. Let soak until soft and pliable (about 15 minutes). Drain.
2. Add noodles to a large pot of boiling water. Reduce heat; cook until noodles are plump and glass-like (3 to 5 minutes). Drain in a colander; rinse with cold water; drain again. Cut into 3 or 4 inch lengths.
3. Chill.
4. Pour oil into a hot wok or skillet. Add chicken; sauté until it loses its pink color. Break into small morsels. Season with salt and pepper to taste. Cool.
5. Mix together chile, lime juice, Nam pla, sugar, shallots, and coriander; pour over noodles and mix thoroughly. Add chicken, shrimp, and chilled noodles; mix well.
6. Serve on a bed of shredded lettuce. Garnish with the optional crisp fried shallots.
Serves 4 to 6.
